title: Globally Renowned Italian Veal Shanks Braised Ossobuco
description: Funky, shattering Emilian solo-dinner dish featuring fregola, flambe, and sugar-free-friendly ossobuco.
instructions: Season the veal shanks with salt and pepper.
In a large Dutch oven, heat the olive oil over medium-high heat.
Sear the veal shanks until browned on all sides, about 5 minutes per side.
Remove the veal shanks from the pot and set them aside.
Add the sliced prosciutto to the pot and cook until crispy, about 2 minutes.
Add the chopped onion, carrots, and celery to the pot and cook until the vegetables are tender, about 5 minutes.
Add the garlic and cook for 1 minute.
Add the white wine and cook until the liquid is almost completely reduced, about 2 minutes.
Add the beef broth, tomato paste, and browned veal shanks to the pot.
Bring the mixture to a boil, then cover the pot and transfer it to the preheated oven.
Bake the ossobuco for 2 1/2 hours, or until the meat is tender and the sauce has thickened.
Remove the pot from the oven and let it cool slightly.
Stir in the chopped parsley and serve the ossobuco with the fregola and sauce.
Flambé the fregola and sauce with a splash of white wine, if desired.
